danger_signals.visual_form: Vehicle exits visible play area on screen edges
danger_signals.meaning: Elimination from race or loss of position; vehicle out of bounds

danger_signals.visual_form: Common objects (furniture, bath items, pool table elements) blocking track
danger_signals.meaning: Collision hazards; track difficulty and environmental danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Opponent vehicles disappearing off-screen due to lead distance
danger_signals.meaning: Losing position; opponent gaining competitive advantage

safety_signals.visual_form: All player vehicles visible within screen bounds
safety_signals.meaning: Active in race; safe from elimination

safety_signals.visual_form: Vehicles visible together on same screen
safety_signals.meaning: In competitive range; not eliminated or left behind

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Vehicles colored differently per team affiliation

faction_indicators.color_identity: Colored lights on screen (one light per player, up to eight)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Light color represents player/character identity in Head-to-Head and multiplayer modes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: All vehicles start at center of play area
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Vehicles positioned closely together; high collision potential

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Vehicles spread further apart as race progresses; screen positioning changes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Leading vehicle gains distance; separation increases between racers

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Division rankings displayed based on point accumulation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Top performers advance divisions; lowest performers relegated

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Colored light indicator changes to match leading player's color
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player achieving sufficient distance from opponents becomes only visible racer

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Loser vehicles disappear from subsequent races
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Eliminated players do not progress to next round

environmental_storytelling.example: Bathrooms and pool tables serve as racing venues with thematic obstacles (bath items, pool balls, furniture) embedded in track design
Top-down perspective and toy vehicle theming (powerboats, helicopters as racing vehicles) communicate game world as miniature/toy scale
Common household objects arranged as track hazards communicate domestic interior settings and constraint-based world design

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Compete for first/second placement; character stats affect their performance
Represents best lap time; ghost competitor for player comparison
Selected character stats only affect computer opponent performance, not player vehicle
Vehicles begin at screen center, progressively separate as race continues
Eliminated from race unless they have traveled furthest; otherwise out of competition

notes: Micro Machines 2 uses primarily positional and color-coded visual communication rather than narrative or symbolic faction systems. The game world is communicated through environmental theming (household/toy scale settings) rather than political or factional conflict. Danger and safety are expressed through screen-space mechanics: on-screen = safe/active, off-screen = danger/elimination. Team identification in Share Games uses simple color-coding. The game's world-state transitions are mechanically driven (race progression, league promotion) rather than environmentally signaled.
sources_used: wikipedia - Micro Machines 2: Turbo Tournament gameplay and mechanics sections
